panels.title: All Probes — Status Table
panels.description:
Current status of every configured probe target. Sorted by Status so failures appear first.

Column legend:
- Status: probe outcome from probe_success. OK = succeeded, FAIL = failed. On FAIL, compare Total Probe Time against the target timeout to distinguish a full timeout from a fast fail (DNS/socket error).
- Name / Probe / Path: target identity. Path = YES means the probe goes through an HTTP proxy (network_path=proxy); blank = direct.
- Primary Latency: probe-type-specific latency (ICMP/MTU RTT, TCP connect, HTTP TTFB, TLS handshake, proxy CONNECT, DNS resolve). See Latency Signal for which metric produced the value. N/A = no usable timing signal (e.g., 100% ICMP packet loss).
- Latency Signal: which metric or phase produced Primary Latency: icmp_rtt, tcp_connect, ttfb, tls_handshake, proxy_connect, or dns_resolve.
- Total Probe Time: wall-clock duration of the full probe (probe_duration_seconds). For ICMP/MTU this runs in seconds by design, driven by ping intervals and retry budgets, not network speed. Not a health signal on its own; use Status + Primary Latency for health.

targets.expr: label_replace(probe_icmp_avg_rtt_seconds{job=~"$job"}, "phase", "icmp_rtt", "__name__", ".*") or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp", phase="tcp_connect"} or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="ttfb"} or (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="tls_handshake"} unless on(target_name) probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="ttfb"}) or (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="tcp_connect"} unless on(target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="ttfb"} or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="tls_handshake"})) or (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="dns_resolve"} unless on(target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="ttfb"} or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="tls_handshake"} or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", phase="tcp_connect"})) or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tls_cert", phase="tls_handshake"} or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="proxy", phase="proxy_connect"} or label_replace(probe_dns_resolve_seconds{job=~"$job"}, "phase", "dns_resolve", "__name__", ".*")
count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", network_path="direct"} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.title: HTTP Status Codes (Direct)
panels.description: HTTP response status codes for direct-path HTTP probes. The series is present only when an HTTP response was received; connection, DNS, TLS, or timeout failures appear as no status-code sample.

panels.title: HTTP Response Truncated
panels.description: 1 means the HTTP response body exceeded the effective transfer limit. Truncation does not fail normal HTTP probes by itself. The series is present only when truncation could actually be evaluated from a received HTTP response.

panels.title: Slowest HTTP Probes (topk 10)
panels.description: Top 10 slowest HTTP probes by total duration, combining direct and proxy-path targets. Use this to spot the slowest endpoints at a glance.

panels.title: HTTP Phase Breakdown (current)
panels.description: Instant snapshot of all HTTP phase durations pivoted by target. Cell colours: green <50 ms, yellow 50–200 ms, red >200 ms. Identifies which phase is slow for each target.

panels.title: HTTP Total Duration (last)
panels.description: Horizontal bar gauge of total HTTP probe duration per target. Thresholds match the phase breakdown table: green <50 ms, yellow 50–200 ms, red >200 ms.

panels.title: ICMP Avg RTT
panels.description: Average ICMP echo round-trip time per target. The series is present only when at least one ICMP echo reply was observed during the latest probe result.

panels.title: ICMP Packet Loss
panels.description: Packet loss ratio per target (0.0 = no loss, 1.0 = 100% loss). Calculated as (ping_count - received) / ping_count for each probe cycle. If the probe context expires before all pings are sent, unsent pings count as lost.

panels.title: ICMP RTT Stddev
panels.description: Population standard deviation of ICMP echo RTT. Values near zero are normal and indicate a stable network path. High stddev with normal average RTT indicates jitter or an unstable path.

panels.title: All MTU Probe States
panels.description: Current MTU probe result per target. Status is the binary probe outcome from probe_success: OK = probe passed, FAIL = probe failed. State is the MTU-specific diagnosis: ok = path MTU detected, degraded = below expected minimum, unreachable = all sizes failed, error = probe error. Detail explains the reason (e.g. fragmentation_needed, largest_size_confirmed).

panels.title: Path MTU per Target
panels.description: Detected path MTU in bytes over time. Calculated as largest successful ICMP payload + 28 bytes (IP + ICMP headers). Standard Ethernet = 1500, WireGuard typical = 1420.

panels.title: MTU Current State
panels.description: Current MTU probe state counts by state/detail.

panels.title: Probes Exceeding Interval (skipped cycles)
panels.description: Counts how often a probe was still running when the next scheduled cycle fired. Non-zero values mean the probe duration exceeds the configured interval — consider increasing the interval or timeout for affected targets.

panels.title: DNS Result Match
panels.description: Shows whether DNS resolution results match the configured dns_expected values. 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. Only populated for targets with dns_expected configured. Useful for detecting DNS hijacking or unexpected record changes.

panels.title: TLS Certificate Expiry — Earliest
panels.description: Days until the earliest TLS peer certificate expiry observed in the latest probe result. Targets where the latest probe failed without retrieving a certificate do not appear in this table.

panels.title: TLS Certificate Expiry — Chain
panels.description: Days until each certificate in the peer chain expires, including cert_index and cert_role labels from the latest probe result.

panels.title: HTTP Body Match
panels.description: Shows whether the HTTP response body matches the configured pattern (body_match_string or body_match_regex). 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. The series is present only when body evaluation actually completed; request/response failures appear as no sample.

panels.title: HTTP Body Status Codes
panels.description: HTTP status codes for HTTP body probes. The series is present only when an HTTP response was received; request, DNS, TLS, or timeout failures appear as no status-code sample.

targets.expr: count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", network_path="proxy"} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Duration
panels.description: Total wall-clock time for the CONNECT tunnel establishment per proxy target, including TCP dial to proxy and CONNECT handshake.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Status per Target
panels.description: Current status of each proxy CONNECT target. OK = proxy accepted the tunnel, FAIL = proxy rejected or connection failed. Sorted by status so failures appear first.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Phase Timing
panels.description: Phases observed in the latest CONNECT probe result: proxy_dial (TCP to proxy), proxy_tls (TLS with proxy, if HTTPS proxy), proxy_connect (CONNECT request/response). Shown for both successful and failed probes; missing phases mean the probe did not reach that phase.
